Winter storms and blizzards can bring extreme cold, freezing rain, snow, ice, and high winds.

metadata.section_header: Take an Active Role in Your Safety
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text:
## STORM


Winter storms create a higher risk of car accidents, hypothermia, frostbite, carbon monoxide poisoning, and heart attacks from overexertion.

metadata.section_header: STORM
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text:
## IF YOU ARE UNDER A WINTER STORM WARNING, FIND SHELTER RIGHT AWAY


Stay off roads.

Stay indoors and dress warmly.

Prepare for power outages.

Use generators outside only.

Pay attention to emergency information and alerts.

Look for signs of hypothermia and frostbite.

Check on neighbors.

metadata.section_header: IF YOU ARE UNDER A WINTER STORM WARNING, FIND SHELTER RIGHT AWAY
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: ## HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: Know your area's risk for winter storms. Extreme winter weather can leave communities without utilities or other services for long periods of time.

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: Prepare your home to keep out the cold with insulation, caulking, and weather stripping. Learn how to keep pipes from freezing. Install and test smoke alarms and carbon monoxide detectors with battery backups.

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: Pay attention to weather reports and warnings of freezing weather and winter storms. Sign up for your community's warning system. The Emergency Alert System (EAS) and National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) Weather Radio also provide emergency alerts.

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: Gather supplies in case you need to stay home for several days without power. Keep in mind each person's specific needs, including medication. Do not forget the needs of pets. Have extra batteries for radios and flashlights.

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: Create an emergency supply kit for your car. Include jumper cables, sand, a flashlight, warm clothes, blankets, bottled water, and non-perishable snacks. Keep the gas tank full.

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: Learn to identify the signs of and basic treatments for frostbite and hypothermia. For more information, visit: www.cdc.gov/disasters/winter/ staysafe/index.html.

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: Stay off roads if at all possible. If trapped in your car, stay inside.

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: Limit your time outside. If you need to go outside, wear layers of warm clothing. Watch for signs of frostbite and hypothermia.

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: Avoid carbon monoxide poisoning. Only use generators and grills outdoors and away from windows. Never heat your home with a gas stovetop or oven.

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: Reduce the risk of a heart attack. Avoid overexertion when shoveling snow.

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: Watch for signs of frostbite and hypothermia and begin treatment right away.

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: Check on neighbors. Older adults and young children are more at risk in extreme cold.

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: Frostbite causes loss of feeling and color around the face, fingers, and toes.

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: Signs: Numbness, white or grayishyellow skin, and firm or waxy skin

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: Actions: Go to a warm room. Soak in warm water. Use body heat to warm. Do not massage or use a heating pad.

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: Hypothermia is an unusually low body temperature. A temperature below 95 degrees is an emergency.

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: Signs: Shivering, exhaustion, confusion, fumbling hands, memory loss, slurred speech, and drowsiness.

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105

text: Actions: Go to a warm room. Warm the center of the body first-chest, neck, head, and groin. Keep dry and wrapped up in warm blankets, including the head and neck.

metadata.section_header: HOW TO STAY SAFE WHEN A WINTER STORM THREATENS
metadata.distribution_statement: US Government publication - inherently public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105
